revision.text:
{{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|12=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft}} |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|12=draft}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=draft}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|12=draft}}, Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|12=draft}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|12=draft}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|12=draft}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|12=draft}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=draft}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|12=draft}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=draft}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|12=draft}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|12=draft}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|12=draft}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|12=draft}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite web|title=About Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|website=Sōzen-ji Official Website|accessdate=2021-03-02|ref={{sfnref|Official Site - About Sōzen-ji}}}} * {{cite book|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}|volume=Vol. 108 Tama District No. 20|chapter=Kawasaki Village - Sōzen-ji, Yakushi Hall|url={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki - Kawasaki Village}}}}
'''

{{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|12=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft}} |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|12=draft}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=draft}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|12=draft}}, Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|12=draft}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|12=draft}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|12=draft}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|12=draft}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=draft}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|12=draft}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=draft}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|12=draft}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|12=draft}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|12=draft}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|12=draft}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

{{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|12=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft}} |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|12=draft}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=draft}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|12=draft}}, Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|12=draft}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|12=draft}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|12=draft}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|12=draft}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=draft}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|12=draft}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=draft}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|12=draft}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|12=draft}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|12=draft}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|12=draft}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite web|title=About Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|website=Sōzen-ji Official Website|accessdate=2021-03-02|ref={{sfnref|Official Site - About Sōzen-ji}}}} * {{cite book|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}|volume=Vol. 108 Tama District No. 20|chapter=Kawasaki Village - Sōzen-ji, Yakushi Hall|url={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki - Kawasaki Village}}}}
'''

'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|12=draft}}, Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|12=draft}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|12=draft}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|12=draft}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|12=draft}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=draft}}.

* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.

revision.text:
{{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft|12=draft}} |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=draft|12=draft}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|12=draft|12=draft}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|12=draft|12=draft}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=draft|12=draft}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|12=draft|12=draft}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|12=draft|12=draft}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite web|title=About Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|website=Sōzen-ji Official Website|accessdate=2021-03-02|ref={{sfnref|Official Site - About Sōzen-ji}}}} * {{cite book|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft|12=draft}}|volume=Vol. 108 Tama District No. 20|chapter=Kawasaki Village - Sōzen-ji, Yakushi Hall|url={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki - Kawasaki Village}}}}

Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />

Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.

* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

revision.comment: 36 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:宗禅寺 up to rev 101424792
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2015年8月14日 (金) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 宗禅寺 |画像 = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 東京都羽村市川崎2-8-20 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 45|緯度秒 = 06.1 | 経度度 = 139|経度分 = 19|経度秒 = 06.9 |山号 = 医王山<ref name="概要"/> |宗旨 =禅宗、臨済宗 |宗派 = 臨済宗建長寺派<ref name="概要"/> |寺格 = |本尊 = 釈迦牟尼佛像 <ref name="概要">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|title=宗禅寺の概要|website=猫の足あと|language=日本語|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |創建年 = [[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（1615年）<ref name="概要"/> |開山 = 玉岫玄球和尚<ref name="概要"/> |開基 =川崎村民 |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = [[奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場]]第三十三番札所 |文化財 = 薬師堂（羽村市指定有形文化財） 本尊・釈迦如来坐像(羽村市指定有形文化財）<ref name=a>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|title=宗禅寺 ～ 芭蕉の句碑～|language=日本語|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |公式HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |公式HP名 = 臨済宗建長寺派 宗禅寺 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=宗禅寺|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''宗禅寺'''（そうぜんじ）は[[東京都]][[羽村市]]にある[[臨済宗]]の寺院。山号は医王山<ref name="概要"/>。鎌倉の[[建長寺]]を本山、[[あきる野市]]五日市小和田の廣徳寺を本寺とする{{sfn|公式サイト 宗禅寺について}}。 開創は1615年(元和元年）<ref name="概要"/>。1615年五日市廣徳寺より玉岫（ぎょくしゅう）玄球和尚を開山に迎える{{sfn|公式サイト 宗禅寺について}}。開創当初は現在地より多摩川寄り、旧奥多摩街道の堂坂下にあったが、1674年の多摩川の氾濫による水害や、玉川上水開削の影響よって1695年現在地に移転している。創建当時には川崎村には寺院がなく、江戸幕府の政策によって当時の川崎村(現、羽村市川崎、玉川、神明台、双葉町）の村民の[[菩提寺]]として創建された{{sfn|公式サイト 宗禅寺について}}。 歴史的には境内にある薬師堂のほうが宗禅寺の成立よりも古く、ご本尊は釈迦如来ではあるが、山号の医王は[[薬師如来]]のことであり、薬師堂(薬師如来）がお寺の象徴として古くから親しまれている{{sfn|公式サイト 宗禅寺について}}。 10月の第１土曜日には薬師如来大祭が執り行われている。[[奥多摩霊場新四国八十八札所|奥多摩新四国88ヶ所霊場巡り]]第33番札所。
== 境内 == 〇薬師堂（川崎一本木堂）
室町年間に再建。羽村には江戸時代以前建立の建造物が確認されていないため、羽村市内において最も古い建造物である可能性が高い。大洪水災害の折に流れてきた一本の流木から建立されていると云われ、一本木堂と呼ばれる。その災害にて亡くなられた方の菩提を弔うためにお堂が建立されたとの伝承がある。
〇天神様などをまつる三社堂、悪病退散地蔵、庚申塚、経塚、芭蕉句碑などがある<ref name="a" />。
== 行事 == === 年間行事 === * [[2月3日]] - 節分豆まき * 春の彼岸中 - 春の文化展 * [[4月8日]] - [[花祭り]](釈尊生誕祭）、甘茶供養 * [[7月15日]] - 盂蘭盆供養・[[盂蘭盆会|山門施餓鬼会]] * 8月第1土曜日夜‐羽村[[灯籠流し|とうろう流し]](会場は市内・宮ノ下運動公園、主催：とうろう流し実行委員会）令和4年に実行委員会の解散が決定し、消滅した。 * [[10月]]第1土曜日 - 薬師如来大祭 * [[12月31日]] - 除夜の鐘
=== 月間行事 === * 写経会 毎月1日・11日・21日 13 -15時 * 木彫教室第 1・3土曜日 13－16時 * 朝粥坐禅会 毎月第１日曜日朝6時―8時半<ref name=b>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|title=東京都の坐禅会情報一覧|language=日本語|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * 土曜講座(公開講座） 毎月1回 *ヨガ教室 毎月第１、第３木曜日 10時～11時 *リアルてらこや 毎月第２土曜日午前中 主催：[https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ らいむぎハウス]
=== 週間行事 === * 毎週土曜日 子供坐禅会(18時～19時）<ref name=b /> * 毎週土曜日 大人坐禅会(19時半～20時半）<ref name=b />
他にも御詠歌、尺八、俳句の会、手打ちうどん教室、新聞サロン、こころの相談室(タロットセラピー）など
== トピック == ・令和３年より３年間、劇団・[[水族館劇場]]が宗禅寺の駐車場にて特設舞台を作り公演をしていた。
== 文化財 == * 宗禅寺薬師堂(川崎一本木堂）天正年間(室町時代）再建（羽村市指定文化財）<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html 市指定文化財] [[羽村市]]、2021年3月12日閲覧。</ref> 羽村市内最古の建造物 * 本尊釈迦如来坐像および迦葉・阿難像（羽村市指定文化財）<ref name="hamura" />
== アクセス == * [[東日本旅客鉄道 ]][[青梅線]][[福生駅]]徒歩25分あるいは[[羽村駅]]西口より徒歩12分 *東日本旅客鉄道 青梅線福生駅よりタクシーにて8分 * 東日本旅客鉄道[[ 八高線]][[東福生駅]]より徒歩25分、タクシーにて8分 *中央自動車道・八王子インターより25分 圏央道日の出インターより15分 圏央道青梅インターより20分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|title=宗禅寺について|website=宗禅寺公式サイト|accessdate=2021-3-2|ref={{sfnref|公式サイト 宗禅寺について}}}} == 関連文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ108多磨郡ノ20|chapter=川崎村 '''宗禅寺 薬師堂'''|id={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 川崎村}}}}
{{デフォルトソート:そうせんし}} [[Category:東京都の寺]] [[Category:臨済宗建長寺派の寺院]] [[Category:羽村市の建築物]] [[Category:羽村市の歴史]]

{{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

{{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>

* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />

{{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite web|title=About Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|website=Sōzen-ji Official Website|accessdate=2021-03-02|ref={{sfnref|Official Site - About Sōzen-ji}}}} * {{cite book|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|volume=Vol. 108 Tama District No. 20|chapter=Kawasaki Village - Sōzen-ji, Yakushi Hall|url={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki - Kawasaki Village}}}}
'''
'''

The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji|qq=}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>

Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji|qq=}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|qq=}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />

== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fussa Station|qq=}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamura Station|qq=}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Fussa Station|qq=}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

{{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamura, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai school|qq=}} |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|qq=}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai school|qq=}} temple located in {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamura, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji|qq=}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji|qq=}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|qq=}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fussa Station|qq=}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamura Station|qq=}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Fussa Station|qq=}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fussa Station|qq=}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamura Station|qq=}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Fussa Station|qq=}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

{{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamura, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai school|qq=}} |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|qq=}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai school|qq=}} temple located in {{ill|Hamura, Tokyo|ja|羽村市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamura, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with {{ill|Kencho-ji|ja|建長寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji|qq=}} in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|qq=}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bodaiji|qq=}} (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|qq=}} (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Fussa Station|ja|福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fussa Station|qq=}} (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of {{ill|Hamura Station|ja|羽村駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamura Station|qq=}} ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from {{ill|Higashi-Fussa Station|ja|東福生駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Fussa Station|qq=}} (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
