Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite web|title=About Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|website=Sōzen-ji Official Website|accessdate=2021-03-02|ref={{sfnref|Official Site - About Sōzen-ji}}}} * {{cite book|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}|volume=Vol. 108 Tama District No. 20|chapter=Kawasaki Village - Sōzen-ji, Yakushi Hall|url={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki - Kawasaki Village}}}}

{{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|qq=}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] temple located in [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with [[:en:Kenchō-ji|Kencho-ji]] in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広徳寺}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] temple located in [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with [[:en:Kenchō-ji|Kencho-ji]] in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広徳寺}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite web|title=About Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|website=Sōzen-ji Official Website|accessdate=2021-03-02|ref={{sfnref|Official Site - About Sōzen-ji}}}} * {{cite book|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}|volume=Vol. 108 Tama District No. 20|chapter=Kawasaki Village - Sōzen-ji, Yakushi Hall|url={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki - Kawasaki Village}}}}
'''
'''

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q18457919]] Wikidata name: Souzenji-temple [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|qq=}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] temple located in [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with [[:en:Kenchō-ji|Kencho-ji]] in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広徳寺}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite web|title=About Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|website=Sōzen-ji Official Website|accessdate=2021-03-02|ref={{sfnref|Official Site - About Sōzen-ji}}}} * {{cite book|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki|qq=}}|volume=Vol. 108 Tama District No. 20|chapter=Kawasaki Village - Sōzen-ji, Yakushi Hall|url={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki - Kawasaki Village}}}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Rinzai school|qid=Q639625}}|prefecture={{ill|Hamura|qid=Q270465}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.751694444444446|N|139.31858333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q18457919]] Wikidata name: Souzenji-temple [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|qq=|1=Kenchō-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110991919}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] temple located in [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with [[:en:Kenchō-ji|Kencho-ji]] in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広徳寺|1=Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485412}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1574146}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=|1=Aquarium Theater|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548673}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=|1=Aquarium Theater|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548673}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite web|title=About Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|website=Sōzen-ji Official Website|accessdate=2021-03-02|ref={{sfnref|Official Site - About Sōzen-ji}}}} * {{cite book|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 108 Tama District No. 20|chapter=Kawasaki Village - Sōzen-ji, Yakushi Hall|url={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki - Kawasaki Village}}}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Rinzai school|qid=Q639625}}|prefecture={{ill|Hamura|qid=Q270465}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.751694444444446|N|139.31858333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q18457919]] Wikidata name: Souzenji-temple [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|qq=|1=Kenchō-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110991919}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] temple located in [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with [[:en:Kenchō-ji|Kencho-ji]] in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広徳寺|1=Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485412}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1574146}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=|1=Aquarium Theater|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548673}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite web|title=About Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|website=Sōzen-ji Official Website|accessdate=2021-03-02|ref={{sfnref|Official Site - About Sōzen-ji}}}} * {{cite book|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 108 Tama District No. 20|chapter=Kawasaki Village - Sōzen-ji, Yakushi Hall|url={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki - Kawasaki Village}}}}

The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1574146}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=|1=Aquarium Theater|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548673}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宗禅寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625}}|prefecture={{ill|Hamura|WD=Q270465}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.751694444444446|N|139.31858333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q18457919]] Wikidata name: Souzenji-temple [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|qq=|1=Kenchō-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110991919}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] temple located in [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with [[:en:Kenchō-ji|Kencho-ji]] in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広徳寺|1=Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485412}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==

=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.

Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] temple located in [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with [[:en:Kenchō-ji|Kencho-ji]] in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広徳寺|1=Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485412}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|qq=|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1574146}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|qq=|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|qq=|1=Aquarium Theater|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548673}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宗禅寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625}}|prefecture={{ill|Hamura|WD=Q270465}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.751694444444446|N|139.31858333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18457919]] Wikidata name: Souzenji-temple [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|1=Kenchō-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110991919}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] temple located in [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with [[:en:Kenchō-ji|Kencho-ji]] in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広徳寺|1=Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485412}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1574146}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|1=Aquarium Theater|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548673}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宗禅寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625}}|prefecture={{ill|Hamura|WD=Q270465}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.751694444444446|N|139.31858333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18457919]] Wikidata name: Souzenji-temple [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=August 14, 2015 (Friday) 12:02 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |Name = Sōzen-ji (宗禅寺) |image = [[File:Souzenji-temple, Tokyo.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo, Japan |Latitude = 35|Latitude_min = 45|Latitude_sec = 06.1 |Longitude = 139|Longitude_min = 19|Longitude_sec = 06.9 |Mountain_name = Iōzan (医王山)<ref name="overview"/> |Sect = Zen Buddhism, [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] |Subsect = {{ill|Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|ja|臨済宗建長寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kencho-ji school of Rinzai Zen|1=Kenchō-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110991919}}<ref name="overview"/> |Principal_deity = Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha (釈迦牟尼佛像) <ref name="overview">{{Cite web|title=Overview of Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.tesshow.jp/tama/fssahamra/temple_hamra_sozen.html|website=Neko no Ashiato|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Founded = Genna 1 (1615)<ref name="overview"/> |Founder = Gyokushū Genkyū (玉岫玄球) <ref name="overview"/> |Patron = Residents of Kawasaki Village |Pilgrimage_site = {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 33rd temple |Cultural_properties = Yakushi Hall (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property), Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha (Hamura City Designated Tangible Cultural Property)<ref name="a">{{Cite web|title=Sōzen-ji - Bashō's Haiku Monument|url=http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/urawa328/souzenji.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> |Official_HP = https://www.hamura-souzenji.com |Official_HP_name = Kenchō-ji School of Rinzai Zen - Sōzen-ji |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Sōzen-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] temple located in [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with [[:en:Kenchō-ji|Kencho-ji]] in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広徳寺|1=Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485412}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Temple Grounds ==
* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1574146}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.
== Topics == - Since 2021, the theater group {{ill|Aquarium Theater|ja|水族館劇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarium Theater|1=Aquarium Theater|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548673}} has held performances on a special stage set up in the temple’s parking lot.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''': Rebuilt during the Tenshō era (Muromachi period), designated as a Hamura City Cultural Property.<ref name="hamura">[http://www.city.hamura.tokyo.jp/0000001535.html Hamura City Cultural Properties], accessed March 12, 2021.</ref> * '''Main Image: Seated Statue of Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha''', along with statues of Kāśyapa and Ānanda (Hamura City Designated Cultural Property).<ref name="hamura" />
== Access == * '''By Train''': ** 25-minute walk from [[:en:Fussa Station|Fussa Station]] (JR Ome Line) or 12-minute walk from the west exit of [[:en:Hamura Station|Hamura Station]] ** 8-minute taxi ride from Fussa Station ** 25-minute walk or 8-minute taxi ride from [[:en:Higashi-Fussa Station|Higashi-Fussa Station]] (JR Hachikō Line)
* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite web|title=About Sōzen-ji|url=http://www.hamura-souzenji.com/about/|website=Sōzen-ji Official Website|accessdate=2021-03-02|ref={{sfnref|Official Site - About Sōzen-ji}}}} * {{cite book|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 108 Tama District No. 20|chapter=Kawasaki Village - Sōzen-ji, Yakushi Hall|url={{NDLJP|763992/52}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki - Kawasaki Village}}}}

The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
== Events ==
=== Annual Events === * '''February 3''': Setsubun Bean-Throwing Festival * '''Spring Equinox Period''': Spring Cultural Exhibition * '''April 8''': {{ill|Hanamatsuri|ja|花祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanamatsuri|1=Hanamatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1574146}} (Buddha's Birthday) with Sweet Tea Offerings * '''July 15''': {{ill|Ullambana Festival|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ullambana Festival|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} and Segaki Ceremony at the Main Gate * '''1st Saturday of August''': Toro Nagashi (Lantern Floating Ceremony) at Miyano-shita Sports Park (event discontinued after 2022) * '''1st Saturday of October''': Yakushi Nyorai Grand Festival * '''December 31''': Joya no Kane (New Year’s Eve Bell Ringing)
=== Monthly Events === * '''Shakyō (Sutra Copying) Gathering''': 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month, 1–3 PM * '''Wood Carving Class''': 1st & 3rd Saturdays, 1–4 PM * '''Morning Porridge Zazen Gathering''': 1st Sunday of every month, 6–8:30 AM<ref name="b">{{Cite web|title=Zazen Events in Tokyo|url=http://www.rinnou.net/cont_02/kenbetu/13t.html|language=Japanese|accessdate=2015-08-14}}</ref> * '''Public Lectures''': Monthly * '''Yoga Class''': 1st & 3rd Thursdays, 10–11 AM * '''Real Terakoya''': 2nd Saturday mornings, hosted by [https://www.instagram.com/raimugihouse/ Raimugi House]
=== Weekly Events === * '''Children’s Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 6–7 PM<ref name="b" /> * '''Adult Zazen Gathering''': Every Saturday, 7:30–8:30 PM<ref name="b" />
Additional activities include Buddhist chanting, shakuhachi (bamboo flute), haiku gatherings, handmade udon workshops, newspaper salons, and a "Heart Counseling Room" offering tarot therapy.

* '''By Car''': ** 25 minutes from Hachioji IC (Chūō Expressway) ** 15 minutes from Hinode IC (Ken-Ō Expressway) ** 20 minutes from Ōme IC (Ken-Ō Expressway)

'''Sōzen-ji''' (宗禅寺) is a [[:en:Rinzai school|Rinzai school]] temple located in [[:en:Hamura, Tokyo|Hamura, Tokyo]], Tokyo. The temple’s mountain name is Iōzan (医王山).<ref name="overview"/> It is affiliated with [[:en:Kenchō-ji|Kencho-ji]] in Kamakura as its head temple and {{ill|Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja|廣徳寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広徳寺|1=Kōtoku-ji (Akiruno)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485412}} in Itsukaichi, Akiruno, as its branch temple.<ref name="official_site">Official website of Sōzen-ji.</ref>
Founded in 1615 (Genna 1),<ref name="overview"/> the temple welcomed the Zen master Gyokushū Genkyū from Kōtoku-ji as its founding abbot.<ref name="official_site"/> Originally located near the Tama River, it was relocated to its current site in 1695 due to flooding and the construction of the Tamagawa Aqueduct. Sōzen-ji was established as the [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] (family temple) for the villagers of the former Kawasaki Village (now parts of Hamura: Kawasaki, Tamagawa, Shinmeidai, Futaba Town) under the policy of the Tokugawa shogunate.<ref name="official_site"/>
The Yakushi Hall predates the temple’s founding, and although the principal deity is Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha, the mountain name "Iōzan" refers to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (Medicine Buddha), highlighting its historical significance as the temple’s symbol.<ref name="official_site"/> An annual festival dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai is held on the first Saturday of October. The temple also serves as the 33rd site of the {{ill|Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja|奥多摩霊場新四国八十八ヶ所霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okutama Pilgrimage New Shikoku 88 Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

* '''Yakushi Hall (Kawasaki Ippongi-dō)''' Rebuilt during the Muromachi period, it is considered the oldest structure in Hamura City. The hall, known as "Ippongi-dō" (Single Log Hall), is said to have been constructed from a single large driftwood log that washed ashore during a great flood, to honor the souls of those who perished.<ref name="a" />
* '''Sansha-dō''' (Hall of Three Shrines), '''Jizō for Epidemic Dispersal''', '''Kōshin Mound''', '''Kyōzuka''' (Sutra Mound), and '''Bashō's Haiku Monument'''.<ref name="a" />
